article_title: Microsoft Unveils MDASH, a Multi-Model Agentic Security Harness That Tops the CyberGym Leaderboard and Finds 16 Windows Bugs
summary: All direct Kim quotes verbatim, all CVE specifics verified, internal link to Firefox 150 coverage valid. Cybersecuritynews 403 and allowlist gap are non-reader-facing.

content_preview.title: Microsoft Unveils MDASH, a Multi-Model Agentic Security Harness That Tops the CyberGym Leaderboard and Finds 16 Windows Bugs
content_preview.summary: Microsoft's new Autonomous Code Security team disclosed MDASH alongside its May 2026 Patch Tuesday, crediting the multi-model agentic scanner with 16 Windows vulnerabilities — four of them critical RCEs — and an 88.45 percent score on CyberGym.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft used its May 2026 Patch Tuesday cycle to publicly unveil MDASH, a multi-model agentic scanning harness built by its new Autonomous Code Security team. According to the [Microsoft Security Blog](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/12/defense-at-ai-speed-microsofts-new-multi-model-agentic-security-system-tops-leading-industry-benchmark/), MDASH discovered 16 previously unknown Windows vulnerabilities — including four critical remote code execution flaws — a...

recommendations: Consider archive.org fallback in chief:review for cybersecuritynews.com (intermittent 403s).

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong News submission, 944 words. Clear structure: Overview, What Microsoft Announced, Benchmark Results, May 2026 Patch Tuesday context, Availability, What We Don't Know. Editorially honest about which numbers are vendor-reported vs independently verified.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all five reachable snapshots. source-0 (Microsoft Security Blog) confirms 88.45% CyberGym score, the 83.1% next-entry figure, 96% / 100% recall on clfs.sys / tcpip.sys, all four CVE numbers and component breakdown. source-1 (CSO Online) confirms verbatim the Taesoo Kim quote 'The model is one input. The system is the product,' attributes it correctly to him as 'Microsoft vice president for agentic security', and confirms CVE-2026-33827 IPv4 use-after-free and CVE-2026-33824 IKEEXT double-free with the exact technical descriptions used in the article. source-2 (The Hacker News) confirms 138 total CVEs and 'none of them have been listed as publicly known or under active attack'. source-3 (BleepingComputer) confirms 120 CVEs, 17 critical, no zero-days. source-4 (Infosecurity Magazine) confirms Kim quote 'The multi-model agentic scanning harness runs a configurable panel of models' verbatim, plus 17 Critical breakdown. source-5 (cybersecuritynews.com) returned 403 (bot-blocked); its single attributed claim — CVE-2026-42898 in Microsoft Dynamics 365 — is independently corroborated by Infosecurity Magazine (source-4). Internal link to Firefox 150 / Mythos Preview coverage at /article/2026-04/22-firefox-150-ships-with-271-ai-found-vulnerabilities-patched-as-mozilla-declares-defenders-can-finally-win verified.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics verified: 16 bugs / 4 critical / 88.45% / 83.1% / 96%-on-28 / 100%-on-7 / 120 CVEs / 17 Critical / 14 RCEs / 138 wider total / CVE-2026-33827, -33824, -41096, -41089, -42898. Kim's title and quotes both verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, with appropriate skepticism. The 'What We Don't Know' section explicitly flags that the CyberGym ordering and recall metrics are vendor-reported and have not been independently audited.
editor_notes.originality: No prior MDASH coverage. The Firefox 150 / Mythos article from April is appropriately linked as related coverage on the broader 'agentic vulnerability discovery' beat.
concerns: cybersecuritynews.com snapshot failed (403). Its attributed claim (CVE-2026-42898 in Dynamics 365) is corroborated by Infosecurity Magazine, so no unreviewed facts.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission. Five reachable snapshots, every direct quote verified verbatim, every specific traces to a snapshot, the lone bot-blocked source has its single claim independently corroborated. The internal Mozilla/Mythos context-setting is editorially appropriate and the link is valid. APPROVE — the warnings concern bot-access infrastructure, not factual integrity.
